name: DURHAM GRAMMER SCHOOL

surface_od_ft.qualifier: as written: faint fraction '13/4' (or '18/4') floating above an otherwise-blank Surface Level line — datum illegible/effectively absent

units: fathoms/feet/inches, transcribed verbatim — convert ONLY downstream

annotations: pencil: ? Rock Head ? (marked to left margin — top of solid / weathered rockhead)
freestone = sandstone; weathered, in a clay matrix
carbonaceous 'stone' beneath the Low Main coal — read as argillaceous
metal = argillaceous mudstone; 'post girdles' = thin sandstone bands within
post = sandstone; 'metal partings' = thin mudstone bands within
unnamed thin coal between Low Main and Hutton; large ink/punch dot in left margin at this row (not a seam correlation)
unnamed thin coal above the Hutton

intervals.verbatim: Grey post, with metal partings and whin near the top
annotations: post = sandstone; 'metal partings' = mudstone bands; 'whin near the top' = hard band
seam named in original; bottom of hole

notes: Header: 'SECTION OF Old Shaft near Durham Grammar School.' Communicated by 'B.T.S. 2652' (ref no.); Borer 'Wm. Coulson' (William Coulson, Durham well-sinker); One-inch Map 26; Six-inch Map 'Durham 27 NW'; BGS Registration No. NZ 24 SE 1 34, Page No. 1. Surface Level line left effectively blank — only a faint fraction ('13/4?' over '4') floats above it, not a usable OD datum. Depth/Thickness columns cross-check perfectly through the whole log — NO clerk arithmetic slips (final base 27 fath 5 ft 2 in = 167 ft 2 in = 50.95 m, matching the ~50.91 m index depth). Pencil '? Rock Head ?' marks the top of solid at interval 5 (Broken freestone). Two large filled dots in the left margin (intervals ~5/6 and 14) read as ink blots / binder punch-holes, NOT seam letters. Page 2 is a blank continuation sheet: printed 'Thickness / Depth from Surface' column header only, no strata — nothing to transcribe. Two unnamed thin coals (intervals 14, 16) sit between the Low Main and Hutton seams with no pencil correlation.
